---
title: "LRP57: Mechanism model — expressive vocabulary (E) -> word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP57 estimates the adjusted association between expressive vocabulary and word reading,
using all three phase transitions.

::: {.callout-note title="Linear mechanism (estimand note)"}
As in [LRP56](../lrp-rli-mech-056), the mechanism enters as a **linear slope**
$\beta_{\text{mech}}$, not the HSGP curve: the nonparametric $f^E$ curve does not
converge at reporting tier (~200 divergences, unmoved by a higher `target_accept`).
No DAG-required adjuster is dropped to fix this; the mechanism is entered linearly,
which converges cleanly, per the #258 review. The estimand is the **linear** $E \to W$
adjusted association.
:::

## Adjustment set

Observed parents of $E$ (EV) under the revised DAG (#245): $\{A, HS, TR, TE, R, RW, SP\}$.

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly by default ($\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$).
- $TR$, $TE$, $R$ — taught receptive/expressive and standardised receptive
  vocabulary; parents of $E$ that also cause $W$ (measure confounders). $TR$/$TE$
  are **new under the revised DAG**.
- $HS$ (`hs` / `hs_missing`), $RW$ (`erbto`), $SP$ (`deapp_c`) — hearing,
  phonological memory and speech production; covariate adjusters **newly
  required (#245)**.
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline.

$F$, $L$, $P$, $B$ are descendants of $E$ or lie on post-$E$ mediation paths and
are **not** conditioned on.

$GA$ (latent general ability) is **not** adjusted for, and the child random intercept
does **not** stand in for it. Under the usual random-effects independence assumption
$u_{\text{child}}$ absorbs stable residual between-child heterogeneity and the
repeated-measure dependence, but it cannot block $E \leftarrow GA \rightarrow W$ or
isolate a within-child association — an omitted trait correlated with the measured
predictors is precisely what a zero-mean, predictor-independent random effect fails to
remove. Blocking it would require a within/between decomposition, correlated random
effects (Mundlak terms), child fixed effects, or a genuine RI-CLPM; none is fitted
here. **Residual confounding by $GA$ therefore remains**, and $f^E$ is an **adjusted
association**, not a causal effect.

### Interpretation caveats

- NUCA applies to $(E_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$ and is not testable.
- $E_{\text{post}}$ is measured with error; attenuation applies as in LRP56.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
